name: Lyonnaise Oyster Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim root ends and most of the green part from leeks. Reserve the white part plus a small portion of the green. Split into halves and rinse thoroughly between leaves. Cut lengthwise into thin strips, then crosswise into very small cubes. Aim for about 5 cups.
2. Peel potatoes and cut them in half lengthwise. Then cut each half crosswise into very thin slices. Aim for about 4 cups.
3. Heat butter in a large saucepan and add chopped leeks. Cook, stirring often, for about 5 minutes.
4. Add the potato slices and water. Bring to a boil. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
5. Simmer for 20 minutes.
6. Drain the mixture in a colander, reserving both solids and liquid.
7. Heat oil and butter in a heavy skillet. Add bread cubes and cook, stirring, until golden brown. Drain in a sieve.
8. Process the leek and potato solids thoroughly while adding small amounts of the reserved cooking liquid to make a fine puree. Use only enough liquid to achieve this consistency.
9. Combine the puree with the remaining liquid in the saucepan and bring to a simmer.
10. Add cream, salt, pepper, and nutmeg to taste.
11. Add oysters with their liquor and cook briefly, just until the oysters curl.
12. Serve sprinkled with parsley, alongside croutons and cheese.
